---
title: "Figure 2 — Time-lapse analysis of iMGL response to LPS+IFNγ"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
# ---- Project-wide knitr settings ----
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = here::here())
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE,
  cache = FALSE
)

# ---- Output discipline ----
OUT_DIR <- here::here("output")
FIG_PREFIX <- file.path(OUT_DIR, "Figure2")
dir.create(OUT_DIR, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Prevent auto Rplots.pdf when running non-interactively
if (!interactive()) pdf(NULL)
# ---- Transparent background theme patch ----
# Apply before ggsave to make all backgrounds transparent
transparent_theme <- theme(
  plot.background       = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
  panel.background      = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
  legend.background     = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
  legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
  legend.key            = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
)
```

```{r load-pipeline}
# Load shared preprocessing if not already in memory
if (!exists("scores_all") || !exists("dat_image")) {
  source(here::here("R", "load_and_preprocess.R"))
}

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(patchwork)
  library(ggforce)
  library(ggrepel)
})
# ---- Transparent background theme patch ----
transparent_theme <- theme(
  plot.background       = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
  panel.background      = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
  legend.background     = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
  legend.box.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA),
  legend.key            = element_rect(fill = "transparent", colour = NA)
)
```

```{r filter-figure2-subset}
# Figure 2 uses: Vehicle + LPS+IFNγ, IL34+, all timepoints, all three experiments
f2_scores <- scores_all %>%
  filter(
    Stimulus %in% c("Vehicle", "LPS+IFNg"),
    IL34 == "pos"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Stimulus = factor(Stimulus, levels = c("Vehicle", "LPS+IFNg")),
    TimeF    = factor(Time_hr, levels = sort(unique(Time_hr)))
  )

cat("Figure 2 image-level profiles used:", nrow(f2_scores), "\n")
cat("Breakdown (Stimulus × Experiment):\n")
print(table(f2_scores$Stimulus, f2_scores$Experiment))
cat("\nExpected: 39 each (3 experiments × 13 timepoints, with 1 aggregated row per experiment-timepoint × some FOV count)\n")
cat("Actual per-timepoint count (Stimulus × Time_hr):\n")
print(table(f2_scores$Stimulus, f2_scores$Time_hr))

var_exp <- pca_model$sdev^2 / sum(pca_model$sdev^2)
pc1_lab <- sprintf("PC1 (%.1f%%)", 100 * var_exp[1])
pc2_lab <- sprintf("PC2 (%.1f%%)", 100 * var_exp[2])

# Color scheme (kept identical to original Rmd for visual continuity)
TREAT_COLORS <- c("Vehicle" = "#0066CC", "LPS+IFNg" = "#CC0033")
TREAT_LABELS <- c("Vehicle" = "Vehicle", "LPS+IFNg" = expression("LPS+"*IFN*gamma))
```

```{r panel-B-per-timepoint-pca}
# One PCA scatter + 95% ellipse per timepoint.
# Each point = one image-level profile (an FOV in one experiment at that time).
# With n=3 experiments × ~6 FOVs, we now have ~18 points per (stimulus, time)
# instead of ~6 in the old single-experiment version -> ellipses more stable.

times_all <- sort(unique(f2_scores$Time_hr))

make_tp_plot <- function(df_t, t_label) {
  ggplot(df_t, aes(PC1, PC2, color = Stimulus)) +
    geom_point(size = 1.8, alpha = 0.85) +
    stat_ellipse(
      aes(group = Stimulus),
      type = "t", level = 0.95,
      geom = "path", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.7,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = TREAT_COLORS, labels = TREAT_LABELS) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 11) +
    labs(
      title = paste0("Time = ", t_label, " hrs"),
      x = pc1_lab, y = pc2_lab
    ) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
      axis.text  = element_text(size = 12),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
}

plot_list <- list()
for (t in times_all) {
  df_t <- f2_scores %>% filter(Time_hr == t)
  if (nrow(df_t) < 3) next
  plot_list[[as.character(t)]] <- make_tp_plot(df_t, t)
}

# Grid of 13 timepoints
pca_grid <- patchwork::wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 3, guides = "collect") &
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

pca_grid
```

```{r panel-C-distance-over-time}
# Centroid distance between Vehicle and LPS+IFNγ at each timepoint.
# With n=3, we can now also report mean ± SD across the 3 experiments,
# not just a single value per timepoint.

calc_centroid_distance_per_exp <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    group_by(Experiment, Stimulus) %>%
    summarise(PC1 = mean(PC1), PC2 = mean(PC2), .groups = "drop") %>%
    pivot_wider(names_from = Stimulus, values_from = c(PC1, PC2)) %>%
    mutate(distance = sqrt((PC1_Vehicle - `PC1_LPS+IFNg`)^2 +
                            (PC2_Vehicle - `PC2_LPS+IFNg`)^2)) %>%
    select(Experiment, distance)
}

distance_per_exp <- f2_scores %>%
  group_by(Time_hr) %>%
  group_modify(~ calc_centroid_distance_per_exp(.x)) %>%
  ungroup()

distance_summary <- distance_per_exp %>%
  group_by(Time_hr) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_d = mean(distance),
    sd_d   = sd(distance),
    n      = n(),
    se_d   = sd_d / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\nDistance over time (mean ± SE across 3 experiments):\n")
print(distance_summary)

p_dist <- ggplot(distance_summary, aes(x = Time_hr, y = mean_d)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = mean_d - se_d, ymax = mean_d + se_d),
              fill = "grey75", alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_line(color = "#333333", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(data = distance_per_exp,
             aes(x = Time_hr, y = distance),
             color = "grey40", size = 1.4, alpha = 0.6,
             inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_point(color = "#111111", size = 2.8) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = times_all) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Euclidean distance between Vehicle and LPS+IFNγ centroids",
    subtitle = "Mean ± SE across 3 independent experiments",
    x = "Time (hr)",
    y = "Distance in global PCA space"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40")
  )

p_dist
```

```{r panel-D-heterogeneity}
# Ellipse area per (Treatment, Time, Experiment) -> heterogeneity proxy.
# Now we have 3 replicates per condition so we can show mean ± SE.

calc_ellipse_area <- function(df, conf = 0.95) {
  n <- nrow(df)
  if (n <= 2) return(NA_real_)
  cov_matrix <- cov(df[, c("PC1", "PC2")])
  eig <- eigen(cov_matrix)$values
  if (any(eig <= 0) || any(is.na(eig))) return(NA_real_)
  dfn <- 2; dfd <- n - 1
  scale_factor <- sqrt(dfn * (n - 1) / (n * (n - dfn)) * qf(conf, dfn, dfd))
  pi * scale_factor^2 * sqrt(prod(eig))
}

hetero_per_exp <- f2_scores %>%
  group_by(Experiment, Stimulus, Time_hr) %>%
  summarise(
    EllipseArea = calc_ellipse_area(pick(PC1, PC2)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
hetero_summary <- hetero_per_exp %>%
  group_by(Stimulus, Time_hr) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_area = mean(EllipseArea, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_area   = sd(EllipseArea, na.rm = TRUE),
    n         = sum(!is.na(EllipseArea)),
    se_area   = sd_area / sqrt(pmax(n, 1)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\nHeterogeneity summary (mean ± SE):\n")
print(hetero_summary)

p_hetero <- ggplot(hetero_summary,
                   aes(x = factor(Time_hr), y = mean_area, fill = Stimulus)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(0.8), width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = pmax(0, mean_area - se_area),
                    ymax = mean_area + se_area),
                position = position_dodge(0.8), width = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = TREAT_COLORS, labels = TREAT_LABELS) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Temporal dynamics of image-level morphological variability",
    subtitle = "Mean ± SE across 3 independent experiments",
    x = "Time (hr)", y = "Ellipse area"
  ) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey40"),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  )

p_hetero
```

```{r save-panels}
# Save individual panels (with transparent backgrounds)

# pca_grid is a patchwork object -> use `&` to apply theme to all sub-plots
ggsave(paste0(FIG_PREFIX, "_B_pca_grid.png"),
       pca_grid & transparent_theme,
       width = 10, height = 14, dpi = 600, bg = "transparent")
ggsave(paste0(FIG_PREFIX, "_B_pca_grid.pdf"),
       pca_grid & transparent_theme,
       width = 10, height = 14, bg = "transparent")

# p_dist is a single ggplot -> use `+`
ggsave(paste0(FIG_PREFIX, "_C_distance.png"),
       p_dist + transparent_theme,
       width = 8, height = 4.5, dpi = 600, bg = "transparent")
ggsave(paste0(FIG_PREFIX, "_C_distance.pdf"),
       p_dist + transparent_theme,
       width = 8, height = 4.5, bg = "transparent")

# p_hetero is a single ggplot -> use `+`
ggsave(paste0(FIG_PREFIX, "_D_heterogeneity.png"),
       p_hetero + transparent_theme,
       width = 10, height = 4.5, dpi = 600, bg = "transparent")
ggsave(paste0(FIG_PREFIX, "_D_heterogeneity.pdf"),
       p_hetero + transparent_theme,
       width = 10, height = 4.5, bg = "transparent")

cat("\nSaved Figure 2 panels to:", OUT_DIR, "\n")
cat("  - Figure2_B_pca_grid.{png,pdf}\n")
cat("  - Figure2_C_distance.{png,pdf}\n")
cat("  - Figure2_D_heterogeneity.{png,pdf}\n")
```
